articles.headline: Lamine Yamal: 'Incredible' to see Lionel Messi still starring at World Cup
articles.description: Spain winger Lamine Yamal has said he is thrilled to see Lionel Messi defy time and continue to have a starring role at this World Cup.

images.name: Lamine Yamal [1296x729]
images.credit: Photo by Grzegorz Wajda/SOPA Images/LightRocket via Getty Images

images.name: Why Spain's substitutes were so important vs. Portugal
images.caption: Alex Kirkland reacts to Spain's late 1-0 win over Portugal to advance to the quarterfinals.

articles.headline: Keir Starmer hints at bank holiday if England win World Cup
articles.description: An England World Cup win could mean an extra bank holiday, Sir Keir Starmer has hinted ahead of a quarterfinal clash with Norway.

images.name: Keir Starmer [1296x729]
images.caption: United Kingdom Prime Minister Keir Starmer and Norway's Prime Minister Jonas Gahr Store greet the media at the Nato summit
images.alt: United Kingdom Prime Minister Keir Starmer and Norway's Prime Minister Jonas Gahr Store greet the media at the Nato summit

images.name: Harry Kane Jude Bellingham [1296x729]
images.caption: Harry Kane (left) and Jude Bellingham (right) celebrate Kane's goal for England on Sunday.

images.name: Odegaard: Norway's quarterfinal against England a 'massive test'
images.caption: Odegaard: Norway's quarterfinal against England a 'massive test'

articles.headline: Morocco star Saibari to miss World Cup quarterfinal vs. France
articles.description: Morocco will be without star forward Ismael Saibari for their World Cup quarterfinal with France on Thursday.

images.name: Ismael Saibari [1296x729]
images.caption: Ismael Saibari suffered an injury in Morocco's win over Canada.
images.alt: Ismael Saibari #11 (C) of Morocco reacts after sustaining an injury during the FIFA World Cup 2026 Round of 16 match between Canada and Morocco at Houston Stadium on July 04, 2026 in Houston, Texas.

article.headline: Mexico kick off World Cup with 2-0 win over South Africa amid three red cards
article.description: Mexico's misery in World Cup opening games was finally ended as they secured a 2-0 win over South Africa at the Estadio Azteca in a match featuring three red cards.
article.linkText: Mexico kick off WC with win over SA ft. 3 red cards

images.name: South Africa manager Broos criticises 'soft' red card in opening game
images.caption: South Africa manager Broos criticises 'soft' red card in opening game.

article.story:
Mexico's misery in World Cup opening games was finally ended as they secured a 2-0 win over South Africa at the Estadio Azteca in a match featuring three red cards.

The co-hosts had been involved in seven matches which either alone, or jointly, opened previous tournaments, but had won none of them.

They put that right at the eighth attempt however as goals from Julián Quiñones and Fulham's Raúl Jiménez in either half gave them a victorious start against a South Africa side who finished with nine men.

Cesar Montes' dismissal in stoppage time took some of the shine off an otherwise upbeat afternoon.

They were a goal up inside 10 minutes when Sphephelo Sithole was caught in possession on the edge of his box by Erik Lira and the ball fell to Quinones, who drove a low shot through the legs of Williams to give the co-hosts a dream start in the ninth minute.

Quinones came close to adding his second with seven minutes of the half remaining, hitting the base of a post from a Brian Gutiérrez lay-off that had Williams beaten.

Sithole's miserable afternoon was complete when he was red-carded for bringing down Gutierrez just outside the box early in the second period, but it took until midway through the half for Mexico to kill off the contest.

Roberto Alvarado stood the ball up to the far post from the right and Jimenez headed in from close range.

South Africa substitute Themba Zwane was sent off after a VAR review for appearing to strike Alvarado in the face late on as the pair tangled, on a day which firmly belonged to the host nation. Brazilian referee Wilton Sampaio produced one more red, sending off Montes in stoppage time for a challenge on Khuliso Mudau.
